channel.title: Bedrock on Cdani's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Bedrock on Cdani's Blog

item.title: What's actually inside a coding agent: Claude Code vs OpenCode vs pi
item.description:
Intro

Lately I keep seeing the same conversation on my timeline: Claude Code is “bloated,” it “wastes tokens,” and a leaner harness like pi is the obvious answer. Then someone else replies that a 28k-token system prompt is the price you pay for a serious coding agent, and the thread devolves into vibes.

I wanted to look at this from a different angle: what is actually being sent to the model on the very first turn? Not benchmarks, not productivity claims — the literal JSON payload that hits the LLM gateway when you say “hello” to each agent.
